block.fluidlogistics.fluid_hatch.tooltip: _Deposits_ _contained fluid_ from your _held item_ into the _tank_ it is placed on. If the held item cannot be emptied, it _fills_ the item from the _tank_ instead.
_Fills_ your _held fluid container_ from the _tank_ first. If it cannot be filled, contained fluid is _deposited_ into the _tank_ instead.

fluidlogistics.configuration.fluidPackag: Maximum fluid amount a fluid package can hold (in mB)
fluidlogistics.configuration.fluidPumpRa: Maximum distance a Fluid Pump can push or pull fluids on either side

Hand Pointer Frogport Batch Edit Limit
Hand Pointer Postbox Batch Edit Limit
fluidlogistics.configuration.fluidTransp: Allow the fluid transporter to extract infinite water from waterlogged leaves
fluidlogistics.configuration.faucetInfin: Allow the faucet and smart faucet to extract infinite water from waterlogged leaves
fluidlogistics.configuration.smartHopper: Allow the smart hopper to extract infinite water from waterlogged leaves
fluidlogistics.configuration.infiniteFlu: Infinite tank capacity (in B/buckets). -1 = follow Create's infinite fluid capacity config option
FOLLOW_CREATE = follow Create's bottomless fluid config option
fluidlogistics.configuration.title: Fluid Package Configuration
fluidlogistics.ponder.fluid_packager_add: When a sign is placed on a Fluid Packager...
Created fluid packages will carry the written lines of text as their address
Package filters route fluid packages based on their address
Fluid packages are destroyed by Mechanical Saws without dropping any items
Besides filters, Package Frogports and Postboxes can also route fluid packages
Check their Ponder scenes to learn how they behave
fluidlogistics.ponder.fluid_packager.tex: Fluid Packagers can package fluids from tanks into fluid packages
Given redstone power, they will extract fluid from the tank and package it up
Fluid packages can be transported and processed like regular items
Inserting a fluid package will unpack it, filling the connected tank
Fluid Packagers will not accept fluid packages they cannot fully unpack
Fluid Transporters can move fluid from one container to another
Fluid Transporters can use filters to restrict which fluids are allowed through
Fluid Transporters can also be disabled by redstone signals
Fluid Transporters can also connect like this
Note that Fluid Transporters cannot connect sideways like this
fluidlogistics.ponder.mechanical_fluid_g: Mechanical Fluid Guns have to be assigned targets before they are placed
Right-Click depots, belts, or other components while holding the Mechanical Fluid Gun to assign them as Targets
Right-Click a selected target again to remove its Selection
Once placed, the Mechanical Fluid Gun will target the blocks selected previously
Mechanical Fluid Guns use the fluid container below them as their fluid source
They can manage multiple targets within range at the same time
Not every fluid container can be interacted with directly
In this situation, Fluid Hatches and Multi-Fluid Access Ports can expose them as targets
fluidlogistics.ponder.multi_fluid_access: Accessing Fluids with Multi-Fluid Access Ports
Accessing multi-fluid containers can sometimes be troublesome
Multi-Fluid Access Ports can extend fluid containers for easier access
Multi-Fluid Access Ports can access fluids in up to three directions at once
Filters can also be configured separately for each of the three directions
Multi-Fluid Access Ports can also be disabled by redstone signals
Note that Multi-Fluid Access Ports cannot be chained
However, one fluid container can connect to multiple Multi-Fluid Access Ports at the same time
fluidlogistics.ponder.faucet.text_1: Faucets can fill items with fluids
fluidlogistics.ponder.faucet.text_2: Faucets choose the fluid intelligently based on the item
fluidlogistics.ponder.faucet.text_3: Unlike Smart Faucets, Faucets cannot fill items on belts
fluidlogistics.ponder.faucet.text_4: Faucets can also fill some fluid containers
fluidlogistics.ponder.smart_faucet.text_: Smart Faucets can fill items with fluids
Filters can restrict which fluid types are allowed through
Smart Faucets can also fill some fluid containers
Smart Faucets can also fill items on belts
Without a filter, Smart Faucets choose the fluid intelligently based on the item
fluidlogistics.ponder.smart_hopper.heade: Transferring Items and Fluids with Smart Hoppers
fluidlogistics.ponder.smart_hopper.text_: Smart Hoppers pull fluids from above and output them toward the spout
Smart Hoppers can also be disabled by redstone signals
Smart Hoppers can also be chained together
Smart Hoppers can transfer items as well as fluids
Smart Hoppers can also use filters to restrict which items and fluids are allowed through
resourcepack.fluidlogistics.cu_again_for: Legacy Copper Textures for Fluid Logistics
